title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: The double iframe pattern nests an iframe within another iframe to work around Content Security Policy restrictions and sandbox escape vulnerabilities. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the application HTML via srcdoc into the inner frame, preventing storage collisions and unauthorized access to parent application data.
writeup: Frédéric Barthelet of Alpic explains the double iframe pattern used by MCP and ChatGPT Apps, where one iframe nests inside another to work around Content Security Policy restrictions and sandbox escape vulnerabilities. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the application HTML via srcdoc into the inner frame. The arrangement enforces origin isolation, preventing storage collisions and unauthorized access to parent application data.
